summary: Completed Phase 1 feature testing for CrossCurrent Hub. Tested Trading Signal Blocking and Version Banner features. All 10 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend verified: Login flow, Trade Monitor page (admin not blocked), Admin Members page with Unblock Signal button in member details Actions tab.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/version endpoint works without auth, returns build_version (UUID format)
GET /api/trade/signal-block-status requires auth, returns {blocked: boolean, missing_days: int, reason: string}
Admins (master_admin, super_admin, admin, basic_admin) are never blocked from viewing signals
POST /api/admin/members/{user_id}/unblock-signal requires admin auth, accepts days parameter (default 7)
VersionBanner component exists in App.js (line 100) and checks version every 60 seconds
Signal-blocked-card UI exists in TradeMonitorPage.jsx (lines 1428-1445) with data-testid='signal-blocked-card'
Unblock Signal button exists in AdminMembersPage.jsx (lines 1215-1240) with data-testid='unblock-signal-btn'
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 features (Trading Signal Blocking + Version Banner) are fully implemented and tested. Backend endpoints: GET /api/version (no auth), GET /api/trade/signal-block-status (auth), POST /api/admin/members/{user_id}/unblock-signal (admin). Frontend: VersionBanner in App.js, signal-blocked-card in TradeMonitorPage, unblock-signal-btn in AdminMembersPage Actions tab.

verified_features.details: Returns build_version (UUID) without authentication. Changes on each server restart.
Requires auth. Returns blocked=false for admin users. Returns missing_days and reason.
Requires admin auth. Accepts days parameter (default 7). Returns unblocked_until date.
Component exists in App.js, checks version every 60 seconds, shows banner when version changes
Admin user does NOT see signal-blocked-card (correct behavior). Card has data-testid='signal-blocked-card'
Found in member details dialog Actions tab. Button has data-testid='unblock-signal-btn' with 'Unblock' text
Login with admin credentials works, returns access_token (not just 'token')
